title: Quoted datum as let/let*/named-let body TAIL inside a define loses its quote — misparse + SIGSEGV
goal: Found by the SICP ch3 corpus (PR #107), verified. A quote literal in tail position of a let-family body INSIDE a define is treated as unquoted: (define (f) (let ((x 1)) 'ok)) => 'Undefined variable: ok' then SIGSEGV; (define (g) (let ((x 1)) '(a b))) => treated as a CALL to a ('Unhandled exception: called undefined function a'). Top-level let is fine; (begin 'ok) wrapping is fine. Same family as #229 (or/and arg dispatch dropped quote/vector tokens) and the letrec* internal-define transform — the define-body/let lowering path drops or re-parses the QUOTE node in tail position.
minimal_repro: (define (f) (let ((x 1)) 'ok)) (display (f))  ;; must print ok; currently Undefined variable + SIGSEGV
acceptance: both repros work (-r AND AOT): 'ok symbol returned, '(a b) list returned
let, let*, letrec, letrec*, named-let tails with quote/quasiquote/vector literals inside defines all correct
no regression: tests/sicp/*, closure_edge, nested_tco, extreme_stress
campaign_payoff: Removes a silent misparse (wrong semantics, not just a crash) hitting the canonical Scheme idiom (let (...) 'done). Third bug found by the SICP gate.
